activities_projects.title: Critical Analysis of the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the deeper meaning of the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the main conflict presented in the poem
Explain how the poem represents challenges in human life
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking and interpretation skills

activities_projects.title: Symbolism Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Understand symbolic meanings in the poem
steps: Identify symbols used in the poem
Explain what the wind symbolizes
Explain how 'weak fires' and 'strong fires' represent human qualities

activities_projects.title: Poetic Device Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze literary devices used in the poem
steps: Find examples of personification and repetition
Write the lines where these devices appear
Explain how they enhance the meaning of the poem

activities_projects.title: Theme Debate
activities_projects.objective: Explore different interpretations of the poem
steps: Divide the class into two groups
One group argues that wind represents destruction
The other group argues that wind represents strength and growth
Write the key points from both sides

activities_projects.title: Comparative Literature Activity
steps: Think of another poem or story about facing difficulties
Compare its theme with the poem 'Wind'
Write similarities and differences

activities_projects.title: Perspective Writing
steps: Imagine you are the wind in the poem
Write a diary entry explaining why you behave strongly
Describe how people can become your friends

activities_projects.title: Philosophical Interpretation
activities_projects.objective: Understand philosophical ideas in the poem
steps: Think about the idea of strength vs weakness
Explain how the poem reflects this idea
Support your answer with lines from the poem

activities_projects.title: Creative Poem Writing
steps: Write a short poem about overcoming difficulties
Use at least one metaphor or personification
Share the poem with the class

activities_projects.title: Real-Life Application Project
activities_projects.objective: Connect the poem to real-world situations
steps: Research a famous person who overcame difficulties
Write how their strength helped them succeed
Relate it to the message of the poem

activities_projects.title: Interpret the Ending
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the conclusion of the poem
steps: Read the last lines about strong fires carefully
Explain why the poet praises the wind
Write how the wind becomes a friend to strong people
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops deeper interpretation of poetic meaning
